title: Cirrus: Fix Success Deps
body: Cirrus-CI recently began failing if dependencies (incorrectly) mentioned the `_task` suffix.  Easy fix.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **vrothberg** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @vrothberg` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/v1.3/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Gating doesn't buy the easy fix @cevich 
or as an old friend mine says, "It's always easy"

History:   I noticed the cron-build for this branch was throwing an `ERROR` (From Cirrus-CI, not the job itself).  Apparently the `.cirrus.yml` file was FUBAR on the branch for quite some time.

The good news is, this PR fixes the YAML so the job can actually start, and the `meta` task can run.  :smile:  The bad news is, now you can [see what happens when `meta` doesn't run in over 3 months](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5620035349643264?command=main#L3-L7) :sob: 

@mheon We can merge this PR safely (it does fix things), unfortunately it also appears that we lost the VM images for this branch. They will need to be manually re-created to fix CI completely on this branch.  Also, it's likely the configuration is pointing the `gate` job at the wrong container image.  This is likely about a week's worth of work :disappointed: 

sigh...20/20 hindsight and all...How important is it to have working CI on this branch?
@baude @mheon what's your opinion?  Want me to spend time recovering the old images and get this branch chooching again?  It might be only a day's work or so (famous last words) for me.
Do we actually care about the v1.3 branch?  I don't and think we should close this.  Or am I misunderstanding what this PR is doing?
Why do we have a 1.3 branch? I don't think we ever shipped 1.3 in RHEL?
Okay, closing this and removing the cron-build as well...
